prompt: Construct a PetMed Operations & Procurement Suite supporting offline-first catalog administration, internal procurement ordering, seat-style inventory locking, and controlled dispatch for warehouse fulfillment teams. The Svelte-based web UI serves three primary roles: Administrator (system setup and audit), Buyer (store employee placing procurement orders), and Dispatcher/Picker (fulfillment staff accepting and executing tasks). Administrators manage pet pharmaceuticals and supplies with SPU/SKU workflows, category/tag browsing, publish/unpublish toggles, primary and detail images, spec attributes (strength, size, flavor), and inventory thresholds that visually warn at 15 units remaining and turn critical at 5 units; staff can bulk import/export via Excel templates and immediately see row-level validation feedback. Buyers build carts from searchable catalogs, with live order estimates that split by supplier automatically, enforce MOQ and pack-size factors (for example, “case of 12” cannot be ordered as 10), and show a final estimate including a configurable handling fee and local sales tax rules where applicable; inventory and pricing are re-checked at checkout and the user is prompted to confirm any change over 2%. For constrained inventory scenarios (such as limited-quantity vaccine appointment “seats” or controlled batch allocations), the UI supports bulk selection and adjacent-seat validation (no single-seat gaps in a row unless the row end is reached), then places a short-term hold for 10 minutes with a visible countdown; holds auto-release on timeout or user cancellation. Dispatchers view a task board that supports both grab-order and assigned modes, with recommended tasks ranked by time window fit, workload balance, and reputation score, and clear feedback when a task is already taken or a hold expired. Reviewers manage “outcomes” records (e.g., internal studies, patents, competition awards, software copyrights) by submitting evidence files and linking them to internal projects with contribution shares that must sum to 100%, along with simple duplicate warnings when titles or certificate numbers closely match existing records.The backend uses Fastify to expose decoupled REST-style APIs consumed by the Svelte frontend over the local network, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for products, suppliers, carts, orders, inventory, task queues, outcomes, projects, evidence archives, and audit logs. Authentication is strictly offline using username and password with salted hashing and an account lockout after 5 failed attempts in 15 minutes; sessions expire after 8 hours of inactivity. Authorization combines RBAC with data-scope constraints so a user can be limited to a specific warehouse, department, or store, and the UI only renders permitted menus/buttons while the API independently enforces the same rules. Concurrency and consistency are enforced through PostgreSQL transactions and row-level locking: seat/lot holds are idempotent per client request key, duplicate orders are rejected, and holds are released by a scheduler every minute; split orders are priced with deterministic rules, and any price/inventory drift triggers a required re-confirmation. Task dispatch uses a queue with optimistic concurrency to prevent double acceptance, and reputation/workload metrics are computed locally from completed task history. Data collection supports form entry, Excel imports with strict format checks, and local “open API” integration for on-prem devices or systems using token auth, request signatures, and rate limiting (for example, 60 requests/minute per token) plus retry with exponential backoff and a dead-letter log after 5 failures. Sensitive fields (password hashes, evidence file paths, certificate numbers) are encrypted at rest where feasible and masked in the UI and audit views, and all files are stored on local disk with checksum fingerprints recorded in PostgreSQL to detect tampering without relying on any external services.
